checks.a_analysis: The output's content was cross-referenced against the source PDF. All substantive paragraphs and headings in the JSON are present in the source document. The document title in metadata is an expected AI-generated field. Page numbers (e.g., elements 17 and 26) are present in the source. No fabricated text or sections were found.

The output contains one URL (http://www.ncgicc.org/Portals/3/documents/Location_of_County_GIS_Operations.pdf) and one physical address (301 North Wilmington Street, 7th Floor Room 770A). Both were verified against the source PDF and are exactly correct. No phone numbers or emails were present in the source to verify.

The source document has 6 pages, all of which are represented in the JSON output across 6 sections. No sections or items (1 through 9) are missing. Continuity was checked at every page break (e.g., from Section 1 to Section 2, Section 3 to Section 4, etc.), and the text flows correctly without omission.
The source document contains no tables.

checks.check_name: Significant text omission within a section
checks.a_analysis: All paragraphs and lists (such as the suggestions in section 5 and the technical questions in section 6) are captured in their entirety. No paragraphs were truncated or omitted within sections.

checks.check_name: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
checks.a_analysis: The text wording, numbers, and dates in the output match the source exactly. Minor formatting changes such as using TeX-style quotes (``inclusions and exclusions'') or combining heading text into the start of a paragraph (reading orders 6 and 8) are acceptable as they do not alter the meaning of the content.

checks.a_analysis: The output document represents the content of the six-page source PDF sequentially. Each section corresponds to a page and its specific content. No paragraphs or sections from the source appear more than once in the structured JSON output.

The heading hierarchy is inconsistent and contains logical breaks. Major sections (1-9) are treated differently: sections 1-3 are paragraphs, section 4 is an H4, and sections 5-9 are H2. This creates a broken hierarchy where H4 (Item 4) is followed immediately by H2 (Item 5), skipping levels and misrepresenting the relationship between peer items. Additionally, subsections of item 3 (b, c, d) are assigned H3, meaning H4 (item 4) appears nested under item 3d, which is incorrect as they are peer sections in the source.
checks.b_evidence_source:
4. Working Group for Professional Land Surveying and GIS
5. GICC Website
checks.b_evidence_output: {"type": "heading", "level": 4, "text": "4. Working Group for Professional Land Surveying and GIS", ...}, {"type": "heading", "level": 2, "text": "5. GICC Website", ...}

checks.a_analysis: The source document does not contain any tables; it is composed entirely of headings, paragraphs, and lists.

The output faithfully follows the reading order of the source document, proceeding from the header through each numbered item and concluding with the adjournment. Page numbers are correctly integrated into the flow.

The conversion fails to preserve the structural integrity of several lists. Specifically, the bulleted lists under '5. GICC Website' (page 4) and the technical questions under Section 6 (page 5) are merged into single paragraphs with simulated bullet characters instead of being represented as proper list structures. This also applies to sub-items 'a' and 'b' under Section 3 on page 1, which are merged into their respective paragraphs.
checks.b_evidence_source:
Suggestions from the committee:
• list the standing committees alphabetically
• add a link to CGIA’s home page...
checks.b_evidence_output: {"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "Suggestions from the committee:\n• list the standing committees alphabetically\n• add a link to CGIA’s home page..."}]}

checks.a_analysis: The URL present on page 3 of the source (pointing to a PDF on ncgicc.org) is captured correctly as text within the paragraph. While not structured as a clickable link object in the output's limited schema, the text content is accurate and no fabricated links were injected.

checks.a_analysis: The HTML output maintains the visual organization and flow of the source PDF. The document's structure as a set of meeting minutes with numbered and lettered sections is preserved correctly in a single-column responsive layout. The transition between pages is handled cleanly, maintaining the textual flow. Normal layout changes for HTML (margins, fonts) are observed but do not impact the organization of information.
The source PDF document consists entirely of text and lists; it does not contain any data tables. Therefore, there are no tables to be misrendered.
The source PDF document does not contain any meaningful images, logos, or charts. It is a text-only document. The HTML correctly reflects this absence.

There is no visual duplication of body content in the HTML rendering. Each paragraph and list item from the source PDF appears exactly once in the body of the HTML. While section titles appear in the Table of Contents, this is a standard and expected accessibility feature for navigation and does not constitute content duplication.
